SUMMARY: NESTED | FIELD | CONSTR | METHOD DETAIL: FIELD | CONSTR | METHOD

javax.media.mscontrol.mediagroup
Interface CodecConstants

public interface CodecConstants

Define the Value constants for the Codecs.

These are used as values for Player.AUDIO_CODEC and Recorder.AUDIO_CODEC correlated to Player.FILE_FORMAT and Recorder.FILE_FORMAT.

Audio Codecs

The audio CODECs available to media operations are the following when setting FILE_FORMAT to FileFormatConstants.RAW, FileFormatConstants.WAV or FileFormatConstants.GSM :

AUDIO_CODEC FILE_FORMAT
FileFormatConstants.RAWFileFormatConstants.WAVFileFormatConstants.GSM
LINEAR_16BIT_128K default, internal, plain format supportedsupportedN/A
LINEAR_16BIT_256K high quality format supportedsupportedN/A
ALAW_PCM_64K common European format supportedsupportedN/A
MULAW_PCM_64K common American format supportedsupportedN/A
ADPCM_32K IMA's Adaptative Differential Pulse Coded Modulation supported (no chunking)supported (no chunking)N/A
ADPCM_32K_OKI OKI's ADPCM supported (no chunking)supported (no chunking)N/A
G723_1B G723.1 N/Asupported (proprietary payload type)N/A
AMR AMR format at Adaptating Rate, used with additional parameters.N/Asupported (proprietary payload type)N/A
AMR_WB AMR wide band formatN/Asupported (proprietary payload type)N/A
G729_A G729 format at 8kb/s N/Asupported (proprietary payload type)N/A
EVRC EVRC format N/Asupported (proprietary payload type)N/A
GSM GSM format N/AN/Asupported

The same CODECs are available to media operations when setting FILE_FORMAT to FileFormatConstants.RAW, FileFormatConstants.WAV or FileFormatConstants.GSM

NOTE: when playing a file with FILE_FORMAT=FileFormatConstants.WAV, the AUDIO_CODEC value is not used as it will be retrieved from the wav file header.

Code snippet for playing Wave files (Wave file made of any of the supported encoding type)
If the file has the traditional extension ".wav", no additional information is required: 
 mediagroup.play("/home/IVR/message.wav", myRtcs, null);
If the file has a non-standard extension, or no extension: 
 params.put(Player.FILE_FORMAT, FileFormatConstants.WAV);
 mediagroup.play("/home/IVR/message", myRtcs, params);
Code snippet for playing raw format OKI encoded files (4 bits per sample, 8000 samples per second)
 params.put(Player.FILE_FORMAT, FileFormatConstants.RAW);
 params.put(Player.AUDIO_CODEC, CodecConstants.ADPCM_32K_OKI);
 mediagroup.play("/home/IVR/myokifile", myRtcs, params);
Code snippet for recording a WAV file (16 bits per sample)
If the file has the traditional extension ".wav", only the codec type is required: 
 params.put(Recorder.AUDIO_CODEC, CodecConstants.LINEAR_16BIT_128K);
 mediagroup.record("/tmp/recorded_message.wav", myRtcs, params);
If the file has a non-standard extension, or no extension, specify also the file format: 
 params.put(Recorder.AUDIO_CODEC, CodecConstants.LINEAR_16BIT_128K);
 params.put(Recorder.FILE_FORMAT, FileFormatConstants.WAV);
 mediagroup.record("/tmp/recorded_message", myRtcs, params);
Note: Using non-standard file extensions is discouraged.

Video Codecs

3gpp and 3gpp2 files are supported. Files with the extension ".3gp" are automatically recognized as 3gpp files, and files with ".3g2" or ".3gpp2" extensions are recognized as 3gpp2 files. The Value FileFormatConstants.FORMAT_3GP can be used to indicate a 3gpp file format, if the file extension is different (similarly to FileFormatConstants.RAW).

Video codecs supported in 3gp files are indicated below.

Code snippet for playing a video file
Video files are played or recorded just like audio files: 
 mediagroup.play("/home/IVR/message.3gp", myRtcs, null);

static Value ADPCM_16K_G726
          G726 16k

static Value AMR
          AMR (narrow band)
static Value AMR_WB

static Value EVRC
          EVRC codec
static Value G723_1B
          G.723 codec
Valid fmtp values, per rfc 3555, paragraph 4.1.3: "bitrate=6.3" or "bitrate="5.3" "annexa=yes" (default) or "annexa=no" Those values may be assigned to Recorder.AUDIO_FMTP.
static Value G729_A
          G.729 codec
Valid fmtp values, per rfc 3555, paragraph 4.1.9: "annexb=yes" (default) or "annexb=no" Those values may be assigned to Recorder.AUDIO_FMTP.
static Value GSM
          GSM codec
static Value H263
          Value for VIDEO_CODEC.
static Value H263_1998
          Value for VIDEO_CODEC.
static Value H264
          Value for VIDEO_CODEC.
static Value INFERRED
          If Player.AUDIO_CODEC is set to this Value, it indicates that the encoding must be read from the file header.
static Value LINEAR_16BIT_128K
          16bits, linear, 8000 samples/second, 128 kbits/s
static Value LINEAR_16BIT_256K
          16bits, linear, 16000 samples/second, 256kbits/s
static Value LINEAR_8BIT_64K
          8bits, linear, 8000 samples/second, 64kbits/s
static Value MP4V_ES
          Value for VIDEO_CODEC.
static Value MULAW_PCM_64K
          Mulaw PCM (mimetype: pcmu)
 

Field Detail

ADPCM_32K

static final Value ADPCM_32K
ADPCM

ADPCM_32K_OKI

static final Value ADPCM_32K_OKI
ADPCM Oki, a.k.a. vox files

ADPCM_16K_G726

static final Value ADPCM_16K_G726
G726 16k

ADPCM_32K_G726

static final Value ADPCM_32K_G726
G726 32k

EVRC

static final Value EVRC
EVRC codec

G729_A

static final Value G729_A
G.729 codec
Valid fmtp values, per rfc 3555, paragraph 4.1.9:
  • "annexb=yes" (default) or "annexb=no"
Those values may be assigned to Recorder.AUDIO_FMTP.

G723_1B

static final Value G723_1B
G.723 codec
Valid fmtp values, per rfc 3555, paragraph 4.1.3:
  • "bitrate=6.3" or "bitrate="5.3"
  • "annexa=yes" (default) or "annexa=no"
Those values may be assigned to Recorder.AUDIO_FMTP.
(use a semicolon as separator)

GSM

static final Value GSM
GSM codec

AMR_WB

static final Value AMR_WB
AMR wideband

AMR

static final Value AMR
AMR (narrow band)

ALAW_PCM_48K

static final Value ALAW_PCM_48K

ALAW_PCM_64K

static final Value ALAW_PCM_64K
Alaw PCM (mimetype: pcma)

MULAW_PCM_64K

static final Value MULAW_PCM_64K
Mulaw PCM (mimetype: pcmu)

LINEAR_8BIT_64K

static final Value LINEAR_8BIT_64K
8bits, linear, 8000 samples/second, 64kbits/s

LINEAR_16BIT_128K

static final Value LINEAR_16BIT_128K
16bits, linear, 8000 samples/second, 128 kbits/s

LINEAR_16BIT_256K

static final Value LINEAR_16BIT_256K
16bits, linear, 16000 samples/second, 256kbits/s

H263

static final Value H263
Value for VIDEO_CODEC. Indicates a H.263 (or H.263-1996) video encoding.

H263_1998

static final Value H263_1998
Value for VIDEO_CODEC. Indicates a H.263-1998 video encoding.

MP4V_ES

static final Value MP4V_ES
Value for VIDEO_CODEC. Indicates a MP4V-ES video encoding.

H264

static final Value H264
Value for VIDEO_CODEC. Indicates a H.264 video encoding.

INFERRED

static final Value INFERRED
If Player.AUDIO_CODEC is set to this Value, it indicates that the encoding must be read from the file header.

If Recorder.AUDIO_CODEC is set to this value, it indicates to use the encoding of the incoming stream, and similarly for Recorder.VIDEO_CODEC.

Note: In all 3 cases it is the default setting anyway; this Value is mostly useful to cancel a previous explicit request for a specific format/codec.
